bullets.text: Fed held rates steady; emphasized inflation focus and uncertainty from Iran war, signaling no rush to support markets.
FOMC projections still imply one rate cut this year, despite energy-price shock risk; path remains data-dependent.
Producer prices rose 0.7% in February (services +0.5%, goods +1.1%), reinforcing upstream inflation pressure.
Iran missile strike damaged Qatar facility tied to major LNG complex; oil jumped on supply-risk fears.
Strait of Hormuz traffic bottlenecks raise shipping and energy-risk premia; any disruption threatens global oil/LNG flows.
U.S. waived Jones Act shipping rules for 60 days to ease domestic fuel logistics and stabilize prices amid conflict.
Powell said he’ll remain Fed chair until successor is confirmed, reducing near-term leadership uncertainty for markets.
Micron revenue nearly tripled and beat estimates as memory demand surged, signaling AI-driven capex and pricing tailwinds in semiconductors.
Pentagon AI contracting: DoD reportedly flagged Anthropic’s constraints as national-security risk while rivals compete for defense workloads.
Meta pivoted from metaverse: shutting Horizon Worlds while paying creators to post on Facebook to boost engagement versus TikTok/YouTube.
Austin’s housing construction surge pushed rents down, highlighting supply expansion as an effective lever against high housing costs.
ACM Turing Award honored quantum cryptography/information pioneers, underscoring strategic momentum in secure communications and quantum tech ecosystems.
New analyses of Antarctic rocks suggest a hidden giant structure beneath the ice, reshaping understanding of continental geology and mantle dynamics.
Qualys disclosed Snap local privilege-escalation flaw (CVE-2026-3888), highlighting persistent Linux packaging attack surface and patch urgency for enterprises.
